---
title: "Tasks"
description: "Step-by-step flow to list, create, update, and delete user tasks (todo / in_progress / done) via the 1Platform API."
sidebar_position: 20
---

# Flow: Tasks

## Overview

This flow documents how a dashboard or productivity widget integrates with the user-task subsystem ÔÇö listing tasks filtered by status, creating new tasks with optional due dates, updating status as work progresses, and deleting tasks when no longer relevant.

**Use case:** Embed a personal to-do list inside a whitelabel dashboard so users can track follow-ups generated by other features (write a missing legal page, review an AI-generated post, renew a domain).

**End result:** The user has a working CRUD interface over their tasks, and other parts of the dashboard can programmatically schedule follow-ups by creating tasks with due dates.

---

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Details |
|---|---|
| App API Key | Application key (`ak-...`) provided when registering your app |
| User API Key | User key (`sk-...`) obtained when creating the user |

### Required headers for all requests

```
Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN
x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN
Content-Type: application/json
```

---

## Variables used in examples

```bash
BASE_URL="https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1"
APP_API_KEY="ak-your-app-api-key"
USER_API_KEY="sk-user-abc123"
APP_TOKEN=""    # Obtained in Step 0
USER_TOKEN=""   # Obtained in Step 1
TASK_ID=""      # Obtained in Step 3
```

---

## Data model

### Task object

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | Yes | MongoDB ObjectId of the task |
| `title` | string | Yes | Short title shown in the list |
| `description` | string | Yes | Free-form description (Markdown allowed) |
| `status` | string | Yes | One of `todo`, `in_progress`, `done` |
| `due_at` | string \| null | Yes | Optional ISO-8601 due date |
| `completed_at` | string \| null | Yes | ISO-8601 timestamp set when status moves to `done` |
| `created_at` | string | Yes | ISO-8601 creation timestamp |
| `updated_at` | string | Yes | ISO-8601 timestamp of the last update |

### Create payload

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | Yes | Task title |
| `description` | string | No | Optional description |
| `due_at` | string \| null | No | Optional ISO-8601 due date (e.g., `"2026-04-30T18:00:00Z"`) |

### Update payload

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string \| null | No | Replace title (omit or send `null` to keep current) |
| `description` | string \| null | No | Replace description |
| `status` | string \| null | No | New status (`todo`, `in_progress`, `done`) |
| `due_at` | string \| null | No | Replace due date |

---

## Step-by-step flow

### Step 0 ÔÇö Get App Token

**Goal:** Authenticate the application and obtain the app JWT token.

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/auth/token`

**Auth:** None (this is the entry point)

```bash
curl -X POST "$BASE_URL/auth/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "'$APP_API_KEY'"}'
```

**Response (200):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "expires_in": 1800
  },
  "msg": "App token generated successfully"
}
```

:::note
Save `data.access_token` as `$APP_TOKEN`. Expires in 30 minutes.
:::

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | Invalid API key or inactive app | Verify that `apiKey` is correct and the app is active |
| 422 | Missing or malformed `apiKey` field | Send `{"apiKey": "..."}` in the body |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded (10 req/min) | Wait and retry |

---

### Step 1 ÔÇö Get User Token

**Goal:** Authenticate the user and obtain the user JWT token.

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/users/token`

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN` (app token only, no user token required)

```bash
curl -X POST "$BASE_URL/users/token" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "'$USER_API_KEY'"}'
```

**Response (200):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "expires_in": 1800
  },
  "msg": "User token generated successfully"
}
```

:::note
Save `data.access_token` as `$USER_TOKEN`. Expires in 30 minutes.
:::

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | Invalid user API key or inactive user | Verify the user's `apiKey` |
| 422 | Missing `apiKey` field | Send `{"apiKey": "..."}` in the body |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded | Wait and retry |

:::tip
If you don't have a user yet, create one with `POST /api/v1/users` sending `{"email": "user@example.com"}`. The response will include the user's `api_key`.
:::

---

### Step 2 ÔÇö List tasks

**Goal:** Page through the user's tasks, optionally filtering by status.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/tasks`

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN` + `x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN`

**Query parameters:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `status` | string \| null | No | Filter by `todo`, `in_progress`, or `done` |
| `skip` | integer | No | Offset for pagination (default `0`) |
| `limit` | integer | No | Page size (default `20`) |

```bash
curl -X GET "$BASE_URL/tasks?status=todo&limit=20" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN"
```

**Response (200):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439011",
      "title": "Revisar art├¡culo \"Gu├¡a SEO 2026\"",
      "description": "Aprobar o pedir cambios antes del viernes.",
      "status": "todo",
      "due_at": "2026-05-02T18:00:00Z",
      "completed_at": null,
      "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "msg": "Tasks fetched successfully"
}
```

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 422 | Invalid `status` value or non-integer pagination params | Use `todo`/`in_progress`/`done` and integer offsets |

---

### Step 3 ÔÇö Create a task

**Goal:** Add a new task to the list, optionally with a due date.

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/tasks`

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN` + `x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN`

```bash
curl -X POST "$BASE_URL/tasks" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "Renovar dominio example.org",
    "description": "El dominio expira el 30/04. Renovar al menos 7 d├¡as antes.",
    "due_at": "2026-04-23T00:00:00Z"
  }'
```

**Response (201):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439020",
    "title": "Renovar dominio example.org",
    "description": "El dominio expira el 30/04. Renovar al menos 7 d├¡as antes.",
    "status": "todo",
    "due_at": "2026-04-23T00:00:00Z",
    "completed_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T15:30:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T15:30:00Z"
  },
  "msg": "Task created successfully"
}
```

:::note
Save `data.id` as `$TASK_ID`. New tasks always start with status `todo` and `completed_at: null`.
:::

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 422 | Missing `title` or invalid `due_at` format | Send a valid title and ISO-8601 timestamp |

---

### Step 4 ÔÇö Update a task

**Goal:** Change the status, title, description, or due date of a task.

**Endpoint:** `PATCH /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}`

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN` + `x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN`

```bash
curl -X PATCH "$BASE_URL/tasks/$TASK_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": "done"
  }'
```

**Response (200):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439020",
    "title": "Renovar dominio example.org",
    "description": "El dominio expira el 30/04. Renovar al menos 7 d├¡as antes.",
    "status": "done",
    "due_at": "2026-04-23T00:00:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2026-04-27T16:00:00Z",
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T15:30:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T16:00:00Z"
  },
  "msg": "Task updated successfully"
}
```

:::note
Setting `status` to `done` automatically populates `completed_at` server-side. Reverting to `todo` or `in_progress` clears it.
:::

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 404 | Task does not exist or belongs to another user | Verify `$TASK_ID` |
| 422 | Invalid `status` value or malformed `due_at` | Use a valid enum value and ISO-8601 timestamp |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded | Wait and retry |

---

### Step 5 ÔÇö Delete a task

**Goal:** Remove a task permanently.

**Endpoint:** `DELETE /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}`

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN` + `x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN`

```bash
curl -X DELETE "$BASE_URL/tasks/$TASK_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN"
```

**Response (200):**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "deleted": true,
    "id": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439020"
  },
  "msg": "Task deleted successfully"
}
```

:::warning
This action is irreversible. The task and its history are removed.
:::

**Common errors:**

| Code | Cause | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 422 | Malformed `task_id` path parameter | Use a valid ObjectId |

---

## Full end-to-end example

Complete flow with consistent fictitious values.

```bash
# === VARIABLES ===
BASE_URL="https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1"

# === STEP 0: Get App Token ===
APP_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/auth/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "ak-prod-app-key-2026"}' \
  | jq -r '.data.access_token')

# === STEP 1: Get User Token ===
USER_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/users/token" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "sk-user-maria-2026"}' \
  | jq -r '.data.access_token')

# === STEP 2: List todo tasks ===
curl -s -X GET "$BASE_URL/tasks?status=todo&limit=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" | jq

# === STEP 3: Create a task ===
TASK_ID=$(curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/tasks" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "Demo task",
    "description": "Sent from the Integration Flows guide",
    "due_at": "2026-05-15T00:00:00Z"
  }' | jq -r '.data.id')

echo "TASK_ID: $TASK_ID"

# === STEP 4: Mark as in_progress, then done ===
curl -s -X PATCH "$BASE_URL/tasks/$TASK_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status": "in_progress"}' | jq

curl -s -X PATCH "$BASE_URL/tasks/$TASK_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status": "done"}' | jq

# === STEP 5: Delete ===
curl -s -X DELETE "$BASE_URL/tasks/$TASK_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" | jq
```

---

## Idempotency and best practices

### Idempotency
- `POST /tasks` is **not** idempotent ÔÇö every call creates a new task.
- `PATCH /tasks/{id}` is idempotent ÔÇö sending the same payload twice produces the same final state.
- `DELETE /tasks/{id}` is idempotent ÔÇö deleting an already-deleted task returns the same outcome (or 404 ÔÇö handle both).

### Retries
- For **5xx** errors on `POST /tasks`, retry with care or use a client-side dedup key.
- For **PATCH** and **DELETE**, retry safely ÔÇö both are idempotent.
- For **429**, back off and retry.
- For **401**, regenerate tokens.

### Timeouts
- All endpoints are simple CRUD ÔÇö set a 5-second client timeout.

### Tokens
- Both tokens (app and user) expire in **30 minutes** (1800 seconds). Implement automatic renewal before expiration.

### Important validations
- `status` must be one of `todo`, `in_progress`, `done`.
- `due_at` must be a valid ISO-8601 timestamp; `null` clears the due date.
- The API automatically sets and clears `completed_at` based on `status`.

---

## Quick endpoint reference

| Step | Method | Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | `POST` | `/api/v1/auth/token` | Get app token |
| 1 | `POST` | `/api/v1/users/token` | Get user token |
| 2 | `GET` | `/api/v1/tasks` | List tasks (paginated, filterable) |
| 3 | `POST` | `/api/v1/tasks` | Create a task |
| 4 | `PATCH` | `/api/v1/tasks/{task_id}` | Update a task |
| 5 | `DELETE` | `/api/v1/tasks/{task_id}` | Delete a task |

---

## Quick checklist

- [ ] I have the App API Key (`ak-...`)
- [ ] I have the User API Key (`sk-...`)
- [ ] I obtained the App Token (`POST /auth/token`)
- [ ] I obtained the User Token (`POST /users/token`)
- [ ] I listed tasks filtered by status
- [ ] I created a task and stored its `id`
- [ ] I updated the task's status through the lifecycle (todo ÔåÆ in_progress ÔåÆ done)
- [ ] I confirmed deletion works via `DELETE /tasks/{task_id}`
